mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in falling corporate earnings and driving negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in >60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment readings.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive returns with a t-statistic above 1.96 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >50% of periods where industrial production increases by more than 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 230.0 reflect entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 20-day returns in >65% of observations when CPI exceeds the 230.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending power and supporting positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: The combined payroll-unemployment signal yields a Rank IC below 0.04 across all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher gross domestic product levels reflect stronger aggregate economic activity that translates directly into higher corporate earnings, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day forward returns, producing a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and elevated consumer prices signals monetary policy lag and stagflation risk, triggering capital flight from equities and causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of observations when low rates coincide with high inflation readings.
